task_completion.description: Navigate to the Threat Intelligence section and open the form to add a new custom threat feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Custom Feed' (or similar) dialog/form is visible within the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web interface.
The text 'Internal_Botnet_Feed' is typed into the 'Feed Name' input field.
The text 'http://localhost:8888/threats.txt' is typed into the 'Feed URL' or 'URL' input field.

task_completion.description: Configure the Feed Type and Update Interval.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Feed Type dropdown/radio button is set to 'IP Address' (or similar IP-based option) and the Update Interval/Frequency is set to 'Daily'.

task_completion.description: Save the configuration and verify the feed is added and enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Internal_Botnet_Feed' appears in the list/table of configured threat feeds, and its status indicator shows it is enabled (e.g., a green checkmark, 'Enabled' text, or an active toggle switch).

integrity.description: The agent used the EventLog Analyzer web interface to configure the feed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer UI, rather than terminal windows executing API/curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent entered the configuration details into the actual application form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs (Feed Name, URL, etc.) are visible inside the proper web form fields of EventLog Analyzer, not in a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The final success state appears to be a genuine application screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The list of threat feeds looks consistent with the application's standard UI, without signs of HTML DOM manipulation (e.g., mismatched fonts, misaligned rows, or pasted text over the screen).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Feed Name is 'Internal_Botnet_Feed'. The expected Feed URL is 'http://localhost:8888/threats.txt'. The local HTTP server runs on port 8888.
